meta.title: California AI Impact Gap Map
meta.subtitle: Three case studies showing where AI investment breaks before it reaches Californians

gapTypes.meaning: We don't know if this works. Nobody is measuring.
gapTypes.policyAction: Commission independent research. Require reporting before expanding.

gapTypes.meaning: The evidence is strong but nobody is deploying.
gapTypes.policyAction: Direct state funding. Create incentives for deployment.

gapTypes.meaning: Both the proof and the funding are missing.
gapTypes.policyAction: Fund pilot programs with built-in measurement.

gapTypes.meaning: The harm chain is strong but no one is tracking it.
The proposed fix isn't working or can't keep up.
gapTypes.policyAction: Restrict or pause until a viable mitigation exists.

examples.title: AI and California's Entertainment Economy
examples.subhead: The same technology. Two opposite outcomes. Nobody measuring which one is happening.
examples.whereItBreaks: Neither side can prove its case. The $750M bet has no AI-specific provisions or measurement. SAG-AFTRA is renegotiating right now with AI as the central issue.

statCallouts.text: California's Film Tax Credit — with no requirement to measure AI's net impact on employment
Of entertainment firms are early GenAI adopters. No independent study separates AI-driven displacement from the 2023 strikes, streaming transition, and production flight.

examples.policyAction: Require AI workforce impact reporting for all productions receiving tax credits. Fund independent measurement (~$3-6M).

benefitChain.name: AI content generation tools are production-ready
Expanded content output grows CA's $175B+ entertainment economy

harmChain.name: Same AI tools displace CA creative workers
Hollywood's workforce hollowed out over a generation

examples.title: AI Wildfire Detection in California
examples.subhead: Detection works. The pipeline from detection to equitable evacuation doesn't exist.
examples.whereItBreaks: No statewide platform connects AI detection to county-level evacuation decisions. The handoff from CAL FIRE to county emergency managers takes 30+ minutes. Non-English-speaking communities were missed entirely.

statCallouts.text: Fires detected by ALERTCalifornia's 1,200-camera AI network in one year. 636 before the first 911 call.
Property destroyed in the January 2025 LA fires. AI cameras were online. Detection occurred. The pipeline to evacuation didn't exist.

examples.policyAction: Fund a statewide integration platform (~$50-100M) connecting AI detection to county evacuation systems. Deploy multilingual alert infrastructure in the 15 highest-risk WUI counties.

chain.name: AI wildfire detection via camera network
Faster evacuation saves lives and property

examples.title: AI and California's Economy
examples.subhead: $203B poured into AI companies. Nobody measuring whether the wealth reaches Californians.
examples.whereItBreaks: AI investment is at record highs while California tech employment is declining. 5.6% unemployment (highest among US states). Nine households control 15% of Silicon Valley wealth. Nobody has calculated the net fiscal impact.

statCallouts.text: Tech stock withholding — roughly 10% of all state income tax. The LAO warns this concentration mirrors the dot-com era.
Total investment specifically measuring whether AI-generated tax revenue exceeds revenue lost from AI-driven job displacement.

examples.policyAction: Fund a net fiscal impact study (~$2-5M) using existing EDD and Franchise Tax Board data. Build a California AI Economic Impact Dashboard (~$1-3M), updated quarterly.

chain.name: Growth generates tax revenue for public services
Public services improve quality of life for Californians

proofOfConcept.intro: California is uniquely positioned to pilot this. You're ground zero for AI development. You already regulate. The framework is domain-agnostic — start with the domains that matter most and expand.
deliverables: A California-specific gap map covering AI's impact on the state economy, healthcare, labor, and education
Per-domain breakdown of where AI is delivering value, where it's falling short, and where harms are going unmeasured
Specific, evidence-graded policy recommendations tied to each gap
proofOfConcept.validationCallout: Four governments (US, UK, Australia, Canada) commissioned a $300M blueprint for evidence synthesis infrastructure. The UK funded £11.5M for METIUS. None are AI-specific. The Gap Map fills that gap.
